stationInfo.name: Station Name
stationInfo.qthGridDesc: Supports 4/6/8 characters, e.g. PM01 or PM01AA. New FT8 operators default to the first 4 characters.
stationInfo.qthLatitudeDesc: Decimal degrees. Leave empty to show grid area on map.
stationInfo.qthLongitudeDesc: Decimal degrees. Leave empty to show grid area on map.
stationInfo.descriptionPlaceholder: Markdown supported. Describe antenna, power, radio model, etc...

description.dxcc: New DXCC entity never worked before
description.grid: New grid square never worked before

display.title: Display & Notification Settings
display.description: Configure FT8 highlighting to quickly identify new callsigns, DXCC entities, and grids.
display.enableHighlightDesc: When enabled, highlights new callsigns, DXCC entities, and grids based on logbook analysis

profileSetup.namePrompt: Give this configuration a name for easy identification and switching:
profileSetup.defaultNameHint: Leave empty to use default name: {{name}}
profileSetup.viewerWelcomeDesc: The station has not been configured yet. Please log in with an admin token to complete the initial setup.
profileSetup.viewerPermissionInsufficient: Insufficient permissions. An admin token is required to configure the station.

radio.pttPortDesc: Specify a dedicated PTT serial port; leave empty to reuse the CAT port, or enter a path manually
radio.noRadioNoTest: No radio mode does not need connection test
radio.testConnectionSuccess: Connection test successful! Radio responded normally.
radio.testConnectionFailedCheck: Connection test failed, please check configuration
radio.testPTTSuccess: PTT test successful! Radio switched TX state for 0.5 seconds.
radio.testPTTFailedCheck: PTT test failed, please check radio connection

testError.AUTH_FAILED: Authentication failed, please check username and password
testError.CONNECTION_FAILED: Cannot connect to radio, please check IP address and port
testError.CONNECTION_TIMEOUT: Connection timed out, please check network and firewall settings
testError.NETWORK_ERROR: Network error, please ensure radio and computer are on the same LAN
testError.INVALID_CONFIG: Configuration error, please check connection settings
testError.OPERATION_TIMEOUT: Operation timed out, please check radio status and retry
testError.UNKNOWN_ERROR: Connection test failed, please check configuration

radio.pttCatDesc: Control via CAT command, suitable for most modern radios (recommended)
radio.pttVoxDesc: Radio auto-detects audio signal, suitable for SignaLink USB and similar interfaces
radio.pttDtrRtsDesc: Control via serial pin, suitable for older radios or external amplifier switching
radio.networkDesc: Connect to an existing rigctld server.
radio.txCompensationNetworkDesc: Compensate for network and radio processing delay to align TX time. Positive values advance TX, negative delay it.
radio.txCompensationSerialDesc: Compensate for serial and radio processing delay to align TX time. Positive values advance TX, negative delay it.
radio.txCompensationNoneDesc: Compensate for audio processing delay to align TX time. Positive values advance TX, negative delay it.
radio.tipLargeWarning: Setting too large (>500ms) compresses decision time and may affect auto-reply
radio.tipAdjustByStats: Use TX delay statistics to fine-tune the compensation value
radio.serialDesc: Connect directly through Hamlib. Depending on the model, this may use a serial port, server address, or device path.
radio.backendEndpointModeDesc: This radio model connects through a Hamlib backend directly, so it needs a device endpoint instead of a local serial port.
radio.deviceEndpointModeDesc: This radio model uses a Hamlib device path directly, so enter the required device path.
radio.serialPortPlaceholder: Select or enter serial port path
radio.serialPortDesc: Prefer selecting a detected port from the list; manual path entry is also supported.
radio.backendEndpointDesc: Enter the device endpoint required by Hamlib for {{model}}, usually an IP, hostname, or host:port.
radio.backendEndpointDescGeneric: Enter the device endpoint required by Hamlib, usually an IP, hostname, or host:port.
radio.deviceEndpointDesc: Enter the device path or system device identifier required by the Hamlib backend.
radio.hamlibBackendConfigDesc: Parameters supported by the selected Hamlib backend are loaded dynamically.
radio.noRigConfigSchema: This model has no extra Hamlib parameters. The address and PTT settings above are enough.
radio.selectRigModelFirst: Select a radio model first to load its Hamlib parameters.
radio.selectRigModelBeforeEndpoint: Select a radio model first to show the correct serial port or server address input.
radio.connectionParamsDesc: These parameters directly affect connection setup and device communication.
radio.multicastParamsDesc: Adjust these only when the selected Hamlib backend uses UDP multicast data or command channels.
radio.backendAdvancedDesc: Adjust these only if you know the backend or radio requires them.
radio.leaveEmptyUseDefault: Leave empty to use the default value: {{value}}.
radio.tipModernRadio: Most modern radios: 9600 baud, 8 data bits, 1 stop bit, no parity
radio.tipOldRadio: Older radios may need: lower baud (1200-4800), 7 data bits, even parity
radio.tipUnstableConnection: Unstable connection: increase timeout, enable retry, add post-write delay
radio.tipPTTIssue: PTT issues: try adjusting RTS/DTR settings
radio.icomWlanDesc: Connect to ICOM radio via WLAN network, supports audio streaming and control
radio.tipIcomWlan: ICOM WLAN mode: typically needs 50-150ms compensation
radio.tipIcomAudio: Audio is provided directly by ICOM WLAN, no separate audio device needed
radio.noneDesc: No radio control, FT8 decode only
radio.tipNoneMode: No-radio mode usually needs no compensation
radio.pageDescription: Configure radio control with a rigctld server, Hamlib Direct Radio, or ICOM WLAN.

audio.refreshFailed: Failed to refresh audio device list
audio.updateFailedGeneric: Failed to update audio device settings
audio.noteInput: Input device: used to receive FT8 audio signals
audio.noteOutput: Output device: used to transmit FT8 audio signals
audio.noteSampleRate: Sample rate: 48kHz recommended for best quality
audio.noteBuffer: Buffer: larger buffer reduces audio pops but increases latency

radioType.noneDesc: No radio connected, FT8 decode only
radioType.networkDesc: Connect to an existing rigctld server
radioType.serialDesc: Connect directly to a supported radio through Hamlib. Depending on the model, this may use a serial port, server address, or device path.

confirmDialog.title: Unsaved Changes
confirmDialog.message: You have unsaved settings changes. Would you like to save them?

modal.title: TX5DR Settings
modal.subtitle: Configure various application settings
modal.radioProfileDesc1: Radio connection and audio devices are configured in the Radio Config Profile.
modal.radioProfileDesc2: Each Profile contains independent radio and audio settings for quick switching between physical radios.

qsoNotifications.title: QSO System Notifications
qsoNotifications.description: Show a system-level notification when a QSO is logged. Notifications only fire while the page is in the background to avoid duplicating the foreground UI.
qsoNotifications.localOnly: This feature is local to the current client only and is never stored on the server.
qsoNotifications.permissionRequiredDescription: The local preference is enabled, but the browser or desktop runtime has not granted notification permission yet.
qsoNotifications.blockedDescription: This site was previously blocked in your browser or system settings. Re-allow notifications there and the feature will resume automatically.
qsoNotifications.unsupportedDescription: System notifications are unavailable because this runtime does not support them or the current origin is not a secure context. Browsers should use HTTPS or localhost.
qsoNotifications.toastEnabledDescription: Future QSO logging events will notify you while the app is in the background.
qsoNotifications.permissionPendingDescription: Permission was not granted, so system notifications remain inactive.

automation.empty: No quick automation actions are available for this operator.
automation.targetSelectionPriorityModeDesc: Controls whether auto-reply prefers new DXCC entities, new grids, or new callsigns first.
automation.priorityModeBalancedDesc: Balance signal quality with grid, DXCC, and callsign novelty.
automation.priorityModeDxccFirstDesc: Priority order: new DXCC > new grid > new callsign.
automation.priorityModeNewCallsignFirstDesc: Priority order: new callsign > new DXCC > new grid.

plugins.title: Plugin Management
plugins.description: Manage automation plugins and load user plugins from the active runtime plugin directory.
plugins.strategyPluginsHelpDescription: A strategy plugin defines an operator's core automation and QSO decision flow. Each operator can use only one strategy plugin at a time.
plugins.utilityPluginsHelpDescription: A utility plugin adds filtering, enhancement, or integration behavior. Utility plugins can stack together without replacing the main strategy.
plugins.emptyLoading: No plugins installed yet. Loading the current plugin directory...
plugins.emptyWithPath: No plugins installed yet. Place plugin folders under {{path}} and reload.
plugins.noGlobalSettings: This plugin has no global settings.
plugins.noGlobalSettingsWithOperatorHint: This plugin has no global settings. Configure it from the operator settings panel instead.
plugins.operatorStrategySettingsHint: Settings for the current strategy plugin.
plugins.logsDescription: Shows plugin loading/reload logs from the host and runtime logs emitted by plugin code.
plugins.logsEmpty: No plugin runtime logs yet. Put plugin folders in the runtime plugin directory, reload plugins, and return here to inspect load attempts and failures.
plugins.directoryHintLoading: Resolving the real plugin directory for the current runtime...
plugins.directoryHintElectron: Put plugin folders here, then come back and reload plugins.
plugins.directoryHintDocker: This is the container path. Mount it to a host folder, then place plugin folders there and reload plugins.
plugins.directoryHintLinux: Administrators can place plugin folders here directly on the server, then reload plugins from the web UI.
plugins.directoryHintDev: This is the real external plugin directory used by the current development runtime on this machine.
plugins.directoryHintGeneric: Place plugin folders here, then reload plugins once the files are ready.
plugins.dataDirectoryHint: Plugin source files and assets stay in the plugin directory, while runtime state and persistent store files are written into the plugin data directory to keep the source tree clean.

serverHealth.title: Server Process Health
serverHealth.coreCapabilityDiagnosticsEmpty: No core capability is marked as unsupported in the current connection session.

openwebrx.introDescription: Connect to OpenWebRX SDR stations to use their audio stream as a virtual audio input device. Once configured, select the station in your Profile's audio input settings. This feature provides audio input only — no SDR control is included.
openwebrx.noStations: No OpenWebRX SDR stations configured yet
openwebrx.urlDescription: WebSocket URL of the OpenWebRX server
openwebrx.addStationFirst: Please add an SDR station first

profileSelect.title: Manual SDR Profile Selection
profileSelect.explanation: No known SDR profile covers the target frequency {{frequency}} MHz. Please manually select a profile that covers this frequency. The system will switch to it and verify the frequency coverage.
profileSelect.botWarning: Warning: OpenWebRX has bot detection. Rapidly switching profiles within 10 seconds accumulates a risk score, which can result in a 12-hour IP ban. Manual selection bypasses the system cooldown, so please choose carefully and avoid repeatedly trying different profiles in quick succession.
profileSelect.verifyFailed: This profile does not cover the target frequency. Actual coverage: center {{centerFreq}} MHz, bandwidth ±{{bandwidth}} MHz. Please select another profile.
profileSelect.verifySuccess: Verified! Profile switched and tuned to target frequency.

clientConfirm.title: Confirm Frequency Change
clientConfirm.message: There are currently {{count}} users on this SDR receiver. Changing frequency may require switching the SDR Profile, which will change the receiver's center frequency and affect other users.

cooldown.title: SDR Profile Switch Queued
cooldown.message: Bot detection cooldown active. The switch will execute automatically in {{seconds}} seconds.

rigctld.title: Rigctld Bridge
rigctld.description: Opens a Hamlib NET rigctl port so N1MM, WSJT-X, JTDX, fldigi and similar tools can reach this radio.
rigctld.lanExposureTitle: Port reachable from the whole LAN
rigctld.lanExposureBody: Any host on the same network can connect. Use only on trusted networks and lock the port down in your OS firewall.
rigctld.clientSetupN1MM: N1MM Logger+: Radio = NET, Port = host-ip:port.
rigctld.clientSetupWsjtx: WSJT-X / JTDX: Rig = Hamlib NET rigctl, Network Server = host-ip:port.
rigctld.clientSetupDocker: Docker / remote: map the TCP port and set bind address to 0.0.0.0.
rigctld.readOnlyOnHint: Reads only (frequency / mode / PTT); every write returns RPRT -11. Use when you just want the logger to record QSOs while you tune manually.
rigctld.readOnlyOffHint: External software can drive the rig (QSY, mode, PTT, split). Needed for click-to-QSY, voice keyer, and spot click-through.
rigctld.readOnlyOffDangerBody: Any client reaching this port can control your rig. Verify the network and the client are trusted; re-enable read-only after the contest.
rigctld.insufficientPermission: Your account cannot manage the rigctld bridge.
